sections.paragraph:
συμφέρω [σύν, φέρω]

—1. ‘bring together so as to form a pile’, heap Ac 19:19.

—2. ‘result in a benefit’, be useful/profitable, be of advantage

—a. as 3 sg. impersonal συμφέρει 1 Cor 6:12; 10:23; 2 Cor 8:10; 12:1 v.l. In some contexts συμφέρει may be rendered it would be good/better Mt 5:29f; 18:6; 19:10.

—b. as nominal ptc., w. art. τὰ συμφέροντα what is beneficial Ac 20:20; πρὸς/ἐπὶ τὸ συμφέρον for what is helpful 1 Cor 12:7; Hb 12:10; without art. οὐ συμφέρον μέν although there’s no profit/point in it 2 Cor 12:1.
